zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Cooper於1370年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾時，她的父親Thomas Cooper41歲，母親Mrs. Thomas Cooper16歲。她於從1388年到1390年與Richard Dickinson在英國越可爾赫爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1441年在英國越可爾享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cooper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker and repairer of wooden vessels such as barrels, tubs, buckets, casks, and vats, from Middle English couper, cowper (apparently from Middle Dutch kūper, a derivative of kūp ‘tub, container’, which was borrowed independently into English as coop). The prevalence of the surname, its cognates, and equivalents bears witness to the fact that this was one of the chief specialist trades in the Middle Ages throughout Europe. In North America, the English surname has absorbed some cases of like-sounding cognates from other languages, for example Dutch Kuiper .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kupfer and Kupper (see Kuper ).
Dutch: occupational name for a buyer or merchant, Middle Dutch coper.

variants.name: Kuiper
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Hendrik, Willem, Harm, Klaas, Kort, Marinus, Nicolaas, Wouter.
Dutch: occupational name for a cooper, Middle Dutch cup(e)re.

variants.name: Couper
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cooper  .

variants.name: Hooper
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a cooper, someone who fitted wooden or metal hoops on wooden casks and barrels, or a barrel-maker, from Middle English hoper, an agent derivative of hop ‘hoop, band’. Compare Cooper .

variants.name: Coop
meanings: English (Lancashire): metonymic occupational name for a cooper, from Middle English coupe, cupe ‘tub, cask’ (from Latin cūpa), probably used for a cooper; compare Cooper . In some cases the surname may have been derived from a pub or house sign.
Americanized form of German or Dutch   Koop  .
History: It was David Koop (his father's surname also spelled Kupe) from Germany whose surname was changed to Coop after he settled in PA toward the end of the 18th century. He later moved to VA.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Cooper was born in 1370, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Cooper, was 41 and her mother, Mrs. Thomas Cooper, was 16. She married Richard Dickinson from 1388 to 1390, in Hull, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1441, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 71.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Cooper(이)가 1370년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Cooper(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Thomas Cooper(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1388년 부터 1390년 까지 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 Richard Dickinson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1441년 영국 요르크실르에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Cooper родилась в 1370 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Cooper было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Thomas Cooper, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Dickinson с 1388 по 1390 в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1441 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год.
